items.summary: DiagnosticsIntegrationTest.skipDiagnostic() is a placeholder that always passes, providing no coverage

evidence: DiagnosticsIntegrationTest line 28: if (!Files.exists(idx)) { ...; return; } — exits before any assertion when index/ is absent
When index/ is present, the method prints a message and calls assertTrue(true) — no real assertion
Comment reads 'This test is now a placeholder'
detail.suggestion: Either delete this test class entirely, or replace it with a genuine integration test that builds a small in-memory index (using @TempDir) and verifies an observable diagnostic property. If the feature tested was removed, remove the test file to avoid dead test noise in the suite.

items.summary: LongIntHashMap, a custom open-addressing hash map used in core indexing, has no tests

evidence: LongIntHashMap.java contains non-trivial rehash logic (lines 100-120), mix64 hash function, and addTo increment semantics
No test file in src/test references LongIntHashMap
The class is used in high-frequency collocation counting loops, making correctness critical
detail.suggestion: Add LongIntHashMapTest covering: addTo with new keys, addTo increment on existing keys, rehash triggered by load threshold (fill past resizeAt), forEach iteration after rehash, and clear() reuse. A parameterized test with a range of initial capacities would also cover the init() rounding logic.

items.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer, WordSketchApiServer, and CollocationsBuilder — the three largest critical classes — have zero test coverage

evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer is 889 lines and implements the core semantic field exploration feature; no test file references it
WordSketchApiServer is 1253 lines and handles all REST endpoints; no test file references it
CollocationsBuilder is 1139 lines and drives all precomputed collocation ranking; no test file references it
SemanticFieldExplorer already exposes SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor executor) for injection, so testing with a mock is straightforward
detail.suggestion: Add unit tests for SemanticFieldExplorer using a mock QueryExecutor (the constructor already supports it): test exploreByPattern with empty/null seeds, verify ExplorationResult fields, and verify minShared filtering. Add API-level tests for WordSketchApiServer using an embedded HttpServer or a test-only Builder wired to a mock executor. Add at least smoke-level tests for CollocationsBuilder against a tiny in-memory corpus.

items.summary: BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest and DependencySketchTest use raw Java assert statements that are disabled unless JVM runs with -ea

evidence: BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest lines 49, 56, 60, 64, 89, 97, 101: 'assert r.getSentence() != null ...', 'assert results.size() > 0 ...' — all raw Java asserts
DependencySketchTest lines 50, 166, 192: 'assert results != null', 'assert results.isEmpty()'
Maven Surefire does not enable assertions by default; these checks are silent no-ops in standard mvn test runs
detail.suggestion: Replace all raw 'assert expr : message' with JUnit 5 assertions (assertNotNull, assertTrue, assertEquals). If assertions are intentionally run with -ea, add <argLine>-ea</argLine> to the maven-surefire-plugin configuration in pom.xml and document it.

items.summary: DependencySketchTest, BlackLabDummyTest, and BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest silently pass when the required index path is unavailable

evidence: DependencySketchTest line 24: if (!indexDir.exists()) { System.err.println("..."); return; } — six test methods all follow this pattern
BlackLabDummyTest does the same for D:\corpora_philsci\bi
BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest uses @EnabledIf("indexExists") on one method but falls back to the same silent-return pattern on the other
Hardcoded Windows path D:\corpora_philsci\bi will never exist in CI
detail.suggestion: Replace silent return with JUnit 5 @DisabledIf or Assumptions.assumeTrue(indexDir.exists(), "Index unavailable") so the test is reported as skipped rather than passing. Move the index path to an environment variable or system property so CI can report the skip reason. For BlackLabDummyTest, which only prints output with no assertions, either delete it or convert it to a proper integration test with JUnit @Tag("integration") and assertion-based verification.

items.summary: TestBCQLParser contains only a main() method and is never executed by JUnit

evidence: TestBCQLParser.java has no @Test annotations and no extends/implements of any JUnit base; it is a plain Java program under src/test
Running mvn test will compile it but execute zero tests from this file
The file tests BCQL parsing — a non-trivial code path — but offers no automated verification
detail.suggestion: Convert TestBCQLParser to a proper JUnit 5 test class: rename to BCQLParserTest, annotate each test case with @Test, replace System.out.println with assertions (e.g., assertNotNull(pattern), assertDoesNotThrow(...)), and remove the main() method.
